---
title: "Geocomputation with R"
subtitle: "Making maps with tmap"
author: "Jannes Muenchow, Jakub Nowosad"
date: 2026-07-06
---

## Setup

Slides and code: <https://github.com/geocompx/user26>

```{r}
#| eval: false
install.packages(c("sf", "terra", "dplyr", "spData", "tmap"))
```

```{r}
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(dplyr)
library(spData)
library(tmap)
```

## R-spatial visualization ecosystem

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="35%" .fragment .semi-fade-out  fragment-index=1}

![](/img/collaborate.png)

R has [a long history](https://r.geocompx.org/intro#history-of-r-spatial) of supporting spatial data processing, analysis, and visualization

:::

::: {.column width="65%" .fragment .fade-in  fragment-index=1}

**Spatial visualization packages include:**

- **rasterVis**, **mapsf**, **ggplot2** and **ggspatial**, **tidyterra**, **rayshader** -- static visualizations
- **leaflet**, **mapview**, **mapdeck**, **leafgl**, **mapgl** -- interactive visualizations
- **tmap** -- both

![](/img/maps.png)

:::

::::

## Anatomy of a {tmap} map

```{r}
#| fig-height: 10
#| output-location: column
tm_shape(nz) +
  tm_polygons(
    fill = "Median_income",
    fill.scale = tm_scale(
      values = "viridis"
      ),
    fill.legend = tm_legend(
      title = "Median income",
      position = tm_pos_out(
         "left", "center"
         )
    ),
    lwd = 0.5
  ) +
  tm_scalebar(
   position = c("right", "bottom")
   ) +
  tm_layout(bg.color = "lightblue")
```

# Code examples

File: [`code/map_code1.R`](../code/map_code1.R)

(Bonus) file: [`code/map_code2.R`](../code/map_code2.R)

## Exercises

Run the code below:

```{r, message=FALSE}
library(sf); library(tmap); library(spData)
```

The `nz` object contains a few attributes of regions within New Zealand.

1. Run the code above. Create a simple map of the `nz` data.
2. Improve the map by coloring regions based on their median income (`Median_income`). Improve the legend title. Try different color palettes (hint: use the `fill.scale` argument). Change border width. Put the legend on the left side.
3. Improve the map further by adding a title, scale bar, north arrow, and text annotation. Adjust the positions of map elements.
4. Switch to `tmap_mode("view")` and recreate the map from step 3. What are the pros and cons of static vs interactive maps?
5. Save the map as `.png` and `.svg` in plot mode, and as `.html` in view mode. (Remember to switch back with `tmap_mode("plot")` for static outputs.)

## Advanced {tmap}

:::: columns
::: column
Website: <https://r-tmap.github.io/tmap/>

Book: <https://tmap.geocompx.org/>

**Other features:**

- Many other visual variables, e.g., `tm_lines()` and `tm_raster()`
- More complex scales for various data types
- Including charts (e.g., histograms) in the legend
- Using basemaps in both static and interactive modes
- Extending {tmap} to other layer types (e.g., [`tm_cartogram()`](https://r-tmap.github.io/tmap.cartogram/articles/cartograms)) or modes, e.g., [`"deck"`](https://r-tmap.github.io/tmap/articles/adv_extensions)
:::

::: column

![](/img/tm_cartogram.png)
![](/img/deckgl.png)

:::
::::

## Other tools {visibility="hidden"}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%" .fragment .semi-fade-out  fragment-index=1}

{leafgl}: <https://r-spatial.github.io/leafgl/>

Allows rendering large datasets (millions of objects) on Leaflet maps by utilizing WebGL technology

![](/img/leafgl.webp)

:::

::: {.column width="50%" .fragment .fade-in  fragment-index=1}

{mapgl}: <https://walker-data.com/mapgl/>

An interface to Mapbox GL JS (API key required) and Maplibre GL JS (no API key required).

Provides support to various map layers (including rasters and heatmaps); allows to create [story maps](https://walker-data.com/mapgl/articles/story-maps.html).

![](/img/dallas.webp)

:::

::::
